key_highlights: Stocks rally to fresh records on easing geopolitical tensions and AI enthusiasm, with oil posting its biggest one-month drop in six years.
AI-driven tech resurgence: 90s tech stars (Nokia, Dell, Cisco) surge, while Atlassian, HPE, IBM, ServiceNow, and Zeta Global post double-digit gains.
Geopolitical crosscurrents: US-China ties show mixed signals (Hegseth praises allies, China warns EU, Taiwan vows not to be silenced), while Strait of Hormuz tensions ease.
Defense and space: SpaceX wins $4B Golden Dome contract and $6.45B Space Force contracts; Hanwha Aerospace eyes European arms deals; Blue Origin rocket explosion boosts SpaceX's edge.
Regulatory and health: SEC moves to repeal climate disclosure rule; Ebola outbreak raises global health concerns; social media companies settle Kentucky lawsuit for $27M.
Oil market under pressure: Exxon warns of breaking point, but consumers benefit from sharp price declines; Dangote cuts fuel prices in Nigeria; Brazil extends fuel price limits.
AI infrastructure and data center growth: Utilities (Xcel Energy, CMS Energy) seen benefiting from data center demand; IBM unveils $15B investments.
M&A and corporate actions: JPMorgan could spend up to $20B on acquisition; Universal rejects Bill Ackman's takeover bid; Paramount pushes LBO debt; BP boardroom drama.
Macro warnings: China factory activity worsens; Britain's bond market sounds alarm; 1 million new car buyers vanish from US economy.
Crypto and digital assets: Michael Saylor says Bitcoin has no competition; Strategy Bitcoin transfer pressures treasury model; China expands digital yuan footprint.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum driven by AI adoption, earnings beats, and strategic investments. 90s tech names (Nokia, Dell, Cisco) are reborn on AI tailwinds. Atlassian, HPE, IBM, ServiceNow, Zeta Global all surged 12-25% on positive updates.
sectors.implications: AI remains the dominant theme. Investors should overweight AI-exposed names but be mindful of valuation extremes (e.g., NVDL decay risk). Data center growth supports utilities and memory stocks (Western Digital).

sectors.trend: Robust demand driven by geopolitical tensions and space investments. SpaceX wins $10.45B in contracts; Hanwha Aerospace eyes European deals; Woodward benefits from aerospace demand. Blue Origin explosion shifts competitive edge to SpaceX.
sectors.implications: Defense spending is secular. SpaceX's IPO anticipation and contract wins make it a key player. Unusual Machines stock gains on drone policy support. ASTS downgraded partly due to Blue Origin's setback.

sectors.trend: Oil prices post biggest monthly drop in six years, providing consumer relief but pressuring producers. Exxon warns of market nearing breaking point. Dangote cuts fuel prices; Brazil extends price limits. Strait of Hormuz tensions ease with US help.
sectors.implications: Lower oil is positive for consumer discretionary and airlines but negative for energy stocks. Watch for OPEC+ response and Iran talks. Windfall tax changes in India add uncertainty.

sectors.trend: Utilities are beneficiaries of data center growth (AI power demand). Truist lowers Xcel Energy target but sees upside; Morgan Stanley trims CMS Energy target. Utility companies are paying consumers to generate power for the grid.
sectors.implications: Data center demand is a structural tailwind for regulated utilities. Grid modernization and distributed generation create opportunities. Defensive positioning with growth upside.

sectors.trend: 1 million new car buyers vanish from US economy, signaling affordability crisis. Mattel stock down 24% on $50.9M exit. Southwest rewinds extra-seat policy. Ferrari Luce aftermarket styling shows luxury resilience.
sectors.implications: Consumer weakness is evident in big-ticket items. Auto and retail face headwinds. Luxury and niche segments may outperform. Southwest policy change reflects operational adjustments.

sectors.trend: Biotech momentum: Tango Therapeutics up 840%, Salubris presents positive ADC data at ASCO. Whitehawk Therapeutics highlights SEZ6 target. Ebola outbreak raises global health concerns.
sectors.implications: ASCO data readouts are catalysts. High-risk/high-reward biotech plays are attracting fund inflows. Infectious disease monitoring is critical for public health and travel stocks.

sectors.trend: JPMorgan CEO Dimon signals potential $20B acquisition, criticizes Coinbase CEO. Visa vs. Mastercard analysis shows competitive dynamics. Corebridge Financial maintained overweight despite lower target. Social media companies settle $27M lawsuit.
sectors.implications: Large banks are consolidating. Payments remain duopoly but face regulatory and competitive pressures. Fintech and crypto regulation are key watchpoints.

sectors.trend: NZ mega house sale highlights luxury real estate agents. US housing affordability remains strained as new car buyers vanish, suggesting broader consumer stress.
sectors.implications: Luxury real estate may be insulated, but mass market housing faces headwinds from high rates and inflation. Monitor mortgage rates and homebuilder sentiment.

key_themes: AI-driven market rally and tech renaissance
Geopolitical tensions: US-China, Taiwan, EU-China trade, Middle East (Hormuz)
Oil price collapse and its macroeconomic implications
Regulatory shifts: SEC climate rule repeal, social media liability, crypto lobbying
Data center growth as a catalyst for utilities and infrastructure
Biotech innovation and ASCO data catalysts
M&A activity and corporate restructuring (JPMorgan, Universal, Paramount, BP)
Crypto and digital assets: Bitcoin dominance, digital yuan expansion

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across regions and sectors; overweight defense and energy security plays; monitor diplomatic developments.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil market disruption (Strait of Hormuz, supply-demand imbalance)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to oil producers; benefit from lower oil via consumer discretionary and transport; hedge with energy ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown and affordability crisis
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid cyclical consumer stocks; focus on essentials, discount retailers, and services; monitor jobs report (headline 95).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI valuation bubble and concentration risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI names; consider equal-weight tech ETFs; be aware of leveraged ETF decay (NVDL).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory uncertainty (SEC climate rule repeal, social media liability, crypto)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Stay informed on regulatory changes; favor companies with strong compliance; avoid speculative crypto plays.
Monitor WHO updates; consider healthcare and biotech exposure; avoid travel stocks if outbreak spreads.
Reduce exposure to China-sensitive sectors (commodities, luxury); focus on domestic demand stories; watch stimulus measures.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Corporate governance and M&A risks (BP boardroom drama, Paramount LBO debt, Universal rejection)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid companies with governance issues; favor transparent management; monitor debt markets for stress signals.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Bond market stress (Britain's bond market alarm)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration risk; favor short-term bonds; monitor central bank policies and inflation expectations.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI infrastructure and data center beneficiaries (utilities, memory, networking)
opportunities.rationale: Data center growth is structural, driven by AI demand. Utilities like Xcel Energy and CMS Energy are well-positioned. Western Digital benefits from memory cycle upswing.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy defense and space stocks on contract wins and geopolitical tailwinds
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX's $10.45B in contracts and Hanwha's European expansion signal robust demand. Unusual Machines benefits from drone policy. Woodward has aerospace growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Take advantage of lower oil prices to buy consumer discretionary and airline stocks
opportunities.rationale: Oil's biggest monthly drop in six years provides margin relief for airlines and consumers. Monitor for further declines.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in biotech with strong ASCO data catalysts
opportunities.rationale: Tango Therapeutics and Salubris show promising data. Whitehawk's SEZ6 target is clinically relevant. High reward potential with proper risk management.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions in 90s tech reborn names (Nokia, Dell, Cisco) on AI tailwinds
opportunities.rationale: These companies are leveraging AI for growth, with strong earnings momentum. Dell's earnings boosted Palantir as well.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to oil producers and energy equities
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil price collapse and Exxon's warning of market breaking point suggest further downside. Producers face margin pressure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against geopolitical risk with gold or defense ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: US-China tensions, Taiwan rhetoric, and EU-China trade disputes create uncertainty. Gold and defense provide safe havens.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid leveraged AI ETFs (e.g., NVDL) due to decay risk
defensive_moves.rationale: High volatility and decay make leveraged ETFs unsuitable for long-term holds. Stick to unleveraged AI exposure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to China-sensitive sectors (commodities, luxury)
defensive_moves.rationale: China factory activity worsens, and trade tensions with EU escalate. Commodities and luxury goods face demand headwinds.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Short-duration bonds or cash as a hedge against bond market stress
defensive_moves.rationale: Britain's bond market alarm and potential rate volatility suggest duration risk. Short-term Treasuries offer safety.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Positive but cautious. The AI-driven rally may continue, but geopolitical risks (US-China, Taiwan, Middle East) and consumer weakness could trigger corrections. Oil's decline supports equities, but watch for OPEC+ response. The US jobs report (headline 95) will be key for rate expectations. Expect volatility around Fed policy and earnings season.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Constructive with selective risks. AI infrastructure spending, defense contracts, and biotech innovation provide structural growth. However, China slowdown, bond market stress, and regulatory changes could weigh. Diversification across sectors and regions is critical. The digital yuan expansion and crypto regulation will shape financial innovation.
key_catalysts: Fed interest rate decision and commentary
US-Iran talks and Strait of Hormuz developments - headlines 27, 73, 79
EU-China trade negotiations and potential retaliation - headline 61
ASCO 2026 data readouts and biotech catalysts - headlines 7, 75
SpaceX IPO progress and contract awards - headlines 14, 59
SEC climate rule repeal implementation - headline 28
Ebola outbreak containment and global health response - headline 33
China stimulus measures and factory activity recovery - headline 47
watch_list: Oil prices and OPEC+ production decisions
US 10-year Treasury yield and bond market volatility
USD/CNY exchange rate and China capital flows
Bitcoin price and crypto regulatory developments
Defense spending announcements from NATO and Asia-Pacific allies
Data center capacity expansion and utility capex plans
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
M&A activity in financials and tech sectors
Global supply chain disruptions (Hormuz, trade policies)
